title: [Crash] OOM killer killing process when using LPAD with large length values
description:
### What happens?

While fuzzing DuckDB, I discovered a crash (segmentation fault or uncontrolled exit) when executing LPAD  functions with a very large integer as the length argument.

### To Reproduce

```sql
SELECT LPAD ( x , 12 , x ) , RPAD ( x , 12 , x ) , UCASE ( x ) , LCASE ( x ) FROM x WHERE x IS NOT NULL ; 
SELECT LPAD ( ) ;
SELECT LPAD ( 1 ) ;
SELECT LPAD ( 1 , 2 ) ;
SELECT LPAD ( 'Hello' , 10 , '' ) ;
SELECT LPAD ( 'a' , 1569325056 , '%d: %s' ) ; 
```

### OS:

Ubuntu 22.04

### DuckDB Version:

22

### DuckDB Client:

Python

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

mao haogang

### Affiliation:

presonal research

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, text_column VARCHAR(255), nullable_column VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, 'Sample Text', NULL);
SELECT LPAD(text_column, 2147483647, 'X') FROM test_table WHERE text_column IS NOT NULL;
key_bug_features: LPAD function with large length argument causes crash
